outlook.headline: KLA and Onto stay tight; Applied remains a share loser
outlook.summary: Process control demand looks healthy, but the next 1-2 quarters still screen as company-divergent rather than uniformly strong. KLA and Onto point to rising relevance, tight supply, and strong visibility, while Applied Materials’ declining share keeps the mix from broadening cleanly; mention traffic is only 0.5x with slight 2% momentum, so the story is steady, not accelerating.

drivers.label: Rising process-control intensity
drivers.detail: KLA says process control relevance is rising across all segments and expects to outperform, implying stronger inspection/metrology content and demand.

drivers.label: Tight supply / execution capacity
drivers.detail: KLA and Onto both cite tight supply, which supports pricing, utilization, and near-term shipment discipline.

drivers.label: Visible backlog and new wins
drivers.detail: Onto’s record backlog and new wins point to solid demand conversion over the next 1-2 quarters.

drivers.label: Share pressure at Applied
drivers.detail: Applied Materials’ declining market share is a relative headwind for this topic and suggests weaker competitive positioning.

risks: Applied’s share losses broaden beyond one company and weigh on the sector narrative.
Tight supply eases faster than demand, reducing the benefit of current backlog and lead times.
Mentions stay flat rather than improving, signaling the theme is not broadening further.
